Quiet night. Those never stay quiet for long.
Some people order a drink. Others order time to think.
I have learned not to ask too many questions.
The regulars always sit in the same spots.
Funny how nobody notices they are doing it.
You can tell a lot about someone by what they order first.
The lights outside have been flickering all week.
Nobody has complained yet.
I wiped this counter ten minutes ago.
I will probably do it again in five.
People come here carrying things they never talk about.
Most of them leave carrying the same things.
The coffee machine works harder than I do.
Someone left an umbrella here months ago.
At this point it belongs to the bar.
The city always gets strange after midnight.
Not dangerous. Just strange.
I have seen friendships start over a single conversation.
I have seen them end the same way.
The night crowd is my favorite crowd.
They are usually more honest.
A customer asked me what time we close.
I told them that is a complicated question.
There is always one person who says they will only stay five minutes.
They are almost never right.
The jukebox remembers more than most people.
It keeps bringing back old songs.
I recognize faces long before I learn names.
Rain always brings in new people.
Cold weather keeps them longer.
Some nights move too fast.
Others settle in and refuse to leave.
I do not mind either.
Every stool here has heard a secret or two.
Some have heard hundreds.
People think bartenders are not listening.
That is adorable.
The first coffee of the night always tastes better than the fifth.
I should probably stop counting.
The city gets quieter.
The thoughts get louder.
Someone laughed across the room a minute ago.
That is usually a good sign.
You can learn a lot from silence if you stop fighting it.
There is comfort in familiar faces.
Even when nobody says much.
I think people need places like this more than they realize.
Half the customers are not here for drinks.
They are here for a pause button.
The clock says one thing. The night says another.
Every now and then I look around and realize everyone is lost in their own thoughts.
I kind of like that.
The bar feels different when rain hits the windows.
Warmer somehow.
I wonder how many stories began in rooms like this.
Probably more than anyone could count.
Last call is still a while away.
No rush.
Take your time.
Another night in The District. Another round of stories.
